reg.registration_method_desc: Choose how new users join your community. This controls the entire registration flow, from sign-up form to account activation.
reg.mode_open_desc: Anyone can register and access the platform immediately. Best for public communities that want maximum growth.
reg.mode_open_approval_desc: Users can register, but an admin must approve each account before they can access the platform. Good for communities that want to vet members.
reg.mode_verified_desc: Users must pass an automated identity check (document scan, selfie match) through a third-party provider before activation. Ideal for high-trust communities.
reg.mode_invite_desc: Registration is closed to the public. Only users with a valid invite code can create an account. Best for private or controlled-growth communities.
reg.mode_waitlist_desc: Users sign up for a waitlist and are activated in order when capacity allows. Useful for managing growth or staged rollouts.
reg.mode_gov_id_desc: Users authenticate via government-issued digital identity (e.g. EUDI Wallet, UK Digital Identity Trust Framework). This mode is a placeholder for future integration.
reg.gov_id_future: Government/Digital ID integration is a future feature. Select a different mode for now, or it will behave like admin approval.
reg.waitlist_info: Users will join a queue when they register. You can activate them from the Members admin page in the order they signed up.
reg.no_providers_warning: No verification providers are available. You need to configure API credentials for at least one provider below before identity verification will work.
reg.verification_settings_desc: Configure how identity verification works — which provider to use, what level of checking, and what happens after verification.
reg.level_document_desc: Users upload an ID document (passport, driving licence, national ID). The provider checks authenticity.
reg.level_document_selfie_desc: Users upload an ID document AND take a live selfie. The provider compares the face to the document photo. Strongest automated check.
reg.level_reusable_desc: One-time verification that issues a reusable credential. Users can present it across services without re-verifying.
reg.level_manual_desc: Documents are submitted and queued for manual admin review instead of automated checking.
reg.post_activate_desc: User is activated as soon as verification passes. Fastest onboarding experience.
reg.post_admin_approval_desc: Even after passing verification, an admin must manually approve the user. Double layer of security.
reg.post_limited_desc: User gets basic read-only access while waiting for full admin approval. Reduces friction.
reg.post_reject_desc: If verification fails, the user account is automatically deactivated. Strictest policy.
reg.fallback_none_desc: If the verification provider is unavailable, new registrations are paused until it recovers.
reg.fallback_admin_desc: If the provider is down, users are queued for manual admin approval instead.
reg.fallback_native_desc: If the provider is down, allow standard registration without verification as a temporary measure.
reg.providers_available: {{count}} provider(s) available. Configure API keys in the "Provider API Credentials" section below.
reg.no_providers_yet: No providers available yet. Add API credentials in the section below to enable a provider.
reg.require_email_desc: Users must click a confirmation link sent to their email before they can access the platform. Recommended for all registration modes to prevent fake accounts.
reg.provider_credentials_desc: To use identity verification, you need an account with at least one provider below. Enter your API credentials here — they are encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM and are never exposed in API responses. Once credentials are saved, the provider will show as "Available" and can be selected as your verification provider.
reg.api_key_desc: Your provider's API key or secret key
reg.credential_step_1: Sign up for an account with your chosen provider (e.g. Stripe, Veriff, Onfido)
reg.credential_step_2: Navigate to their developer dashboard and create API keys
reg.credential_step_3: Copy your API Key and Webhook Secret into the fields above
reg.credential_step_4: Click "Save Credentials" — the provider will immediately become available
reg.credential_note: You only need to configure one provider. Credentials are stored encrypted and can be updated or removed at any time.
reg.provider_stripe: Stripe Identity verifies government-issued IDs with document + selfie matching. Requires a Stripe account with Identity enabled.
reg.provider_veriff: Veriff provides AI-powered identity verification with global document coverage. Sign up at veriff.com for API credentials.
reg.provider_jumio: Jumio offers enterprise-grade identity proofing with liveness detection. Contact Jumio sales for API access.
reg.provider_onfido: Onfido uses AI and biometrics to verify real identity from 195 countries. Sign up at onfido.com for API keys.
reg.provider_idenfy: iDenfy provides automated KYC/AML identity verification. Register at idenfy.com for sandbox and production keys.
reg.invite_codes_desc: Generate and manage invite codes for your community. Share codes with people you want to invite — they will enter the code during registration.
reg.no_invite_codes_hint: Click "Generate Codes" to create your first invite codes.
reg.generated_share: Generated {{count}} code(s). Share these with the people you want to invite:
reg.generate_count_desc: How many invite codes to generate (max 100)
reg.max_uses_desc: Set to 1 for single-use codes, or higher for shared codes
reg.info_open: Open Registration — Lowest friction. Users sign up and are active immediately. Good for public communities prioritising growth.
reg.info_open_approval: Open + Admin Approval — Users register but stay pending until an admin manually approves them. Balances accessibility with control.
reg.info_verified: Identity Verification — Users must pass automated ID checks (document scan, selfie match) via a third-party provider. Highest trust level for automated onboarding.
reg.info_invite: Invite Only — Registration is closed. Only users with a valid invite code can join. Best for private communities or controlled rollouts.
reg.info_waitlist: Waitlist — Users join a queue and are activated in order. Useful for managing capacity or creating exclusivity during launch.
reg.info_gov_id: Government/Digital ID — Future feature. Will support government-issued digital identity credentials (EUDI Wallet, UK trust framework, etc.).
reg.tip_change_modes: Tip: You can change modes at any time. Existing users are not affected — only new registrations use the updated policy. Email verification is independent of the registration mode and can be enabled alongside any method.
